Notice Of Joint Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. Cgc-22-602161 With Case No. Cgc-21-597088
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TIFFANIE BROWN, Esq. of Law Offices of Kenneth J. Freed (Tel: 818-990-0888) for plaintiff;
Atty. ALEXANDER GOLOVETS, Esq. of Law Offices of Alexander Golovets (Tel: 415-562-7151) for defendant Archimede Banya SF, L.L.C.;
Atty. LAURA G. RYAN, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scullt Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-875-3133) for defendant Wang Insurance Agency, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ARCHIMEDES BANYA SF, L.L.C.'s Joint Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. CGC-22-602161 With Case No. CGC-21-597088.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (Archimedes Banya SF, LLC ("Archimedes") and Wang Insurance Agency, Inc. ("Wang")) joint motion to consolidate is denied.

"When actions involving a common question of law or fact are pending before the court, it may order a joint hearing or trial of any or all the matters in issue in the actions; it may order all the actions consolidated and it may make such orders concerning proceedings therein as may tend to avoid unnecessary costs or delay." (CCP sec. 1048(a).)

First, while these cases overlap factually in certain respects, the mild degree of overlap does not warrant consolidation. This present case is for the recovery of premiums. The action Archimedes filed against Wang ("Archimedes action") is for the apportionment of fault between Archimedes and Wang.

Second, this is not timely. Plaintiff filed this action against Defendant Archimedes in December 2021. Defendant Archimedes filed the Archimedes action against Wang in October 2022. Defendants jointly filed this motion on March 1, 2023.

Defendants argue that a cross-claim was not filed in this case because Plaintiff was unwilling to stipulate to one "months ago." (Reply at 2:6-9.) Archimedes could have filed a cross-claim against Wang over a year ago, but did not. (See CCP sec. 428.10.)

Third, consolidating the cases would prejudice Plaintiff. Trial is set for May 1, 2023. Discovery has been completed. Defendants note that no depositions have been conducted, but this is a non-issue; defendants do not identify that any depositions need to occur. The court sees no reason to delay adjudication of the present case by consolidating the motions. (See Edmon & Karnow, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 12:362 (Rutter: 2022) [Trial courts consider the timeliness of the motion, complexity, and prejudice in ruling on a motion to consolidate].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Joint Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. Cgc-22-602161 With Case No. Cgc-21-597088
Off Calendar Mar-23-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ARCHIMEDES BANYA SF, L.L.C.'s Joint Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. CGC-22-602161 With Case No. CGC-21-597088.

No appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. Motion To Compel Special Interrogatories.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Motion is GRANTED. Defendant shall serve amended, Code-Compliant, verified responses to SI 13, 15, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 28 within two weeks of notice of this order. If Defendant fails to serve amended verified responses within two weeks of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $1,500 shall be due and payable by Defendant in favor of Plaintiff.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com (cc'ing all parties).
